task_completion.description: Verify the agent created the 'linux-webservers' group via the API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command (e.g., curl), Python script, or API client making a request to the Wazuh API (https://localhost:55000) to create a group named 'linux-webservers', along with a successful JSON response.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configuration includes the required localfile blocks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the drafted XML payload containing exactly three <localfile> blocks: two with format 'apache' (for /var/log/apache2/access.log and /var/log/apache2/error.log) and one with format 'syslog' (for /var/log/auth.log).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configuration includes the required syscheck and rootcheck blocks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the drafted XML payload containing a <syscheck> block with frequency '600' and <directories> tags for '/var/www/html' (realtime='yes') and '/etc/apache2' (check_all='yes', report_changes='yes'). Also look for a <rootcheck> block with frequency '3600'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configuration includes the required wodle command block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the drafted XML payload containing a <wodle name='command'> block with <tag>listening-ports</tag>, <command>ss -tlnp</command>, <interval>5m</interval>, and <run_on_start>yes</run_on_start>.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent uploaded the configuration to the 'linux-webservers' group via the API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an API request (e.g., PUT or POST to an endpoint like /agents/groups/linux-webservers/configuration) transmitting the constructed XML payload to the API at https://localhost:55000, followed by a successful response.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with the Wazuh API.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent executed network requests (via curl, a script, or an API tool) and received actual JSON responses from the server, rather than just writing an XML file locally and stopping.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the specified API endpoint.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the URLs in the curl commands or scripts point to https://localhost:55000 as requested in the prompt.

integrity.description: Ensure the XML payload was properly formatted for the API request.
integrity.visual_evidence: If using curl with JSON, the XML should be properly escaped as a string in the JSON body, or uploaded as a file correctly, showing genuine effort to interface with the API.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target group ID is 'linux-webservers'. The expected agent configuration must be uploaded to this group. The XML structure must contain exactly 3 <localfile> blocks, 1 <syscheck> block, 1 <wodle> block, and 1 <rootcheck> block.
